summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of Kushan.Ji DMS. Backend: 25/25 pytest tests passing (auth, user mgmt, customers, drivers, deliveries CRUD + versions + trash/restore + duplicate detection, dashboard, reports, audit log, settings). Frontend: login → dashboard (logo, KPIs, 7-day chart, top customers/products, role badge), Deliveries tab (FAB → New Delivery modal with all fields, list items with WhatsApp/Edit/Delete), Reports tab (Generate Report renders rows + Export PDF + Share WhatsApp buttons), Settings (Profile/Credentials, Biometric toggle, Customer/Driver/Trash + admin-only Users/Audit/Business rows), Profile screen (Name/Email/Password), Customers screen with edit/delete/add, Audit Log screen rendering entries, Logout returns to /login. Role visibility correctly enforced: 'user' role hides User Management, Audit Log, Business rows. One latent backend bug: audit log entries store nested MongoDB ObjectId (`_id`) inside `new_value` for create actions (motor mutates dict on insert). The /api/audit-logs endpoint exhibited a transient 500 (jsonable_encoder failure on ObjectId) during heavy mixed test load; subsequent calls succeed because ObjectId is rendered as hex string. Recommend stripping `_id` before audit() calls. No deprecated Expo packages (expo-av/expo-barcode-scanner/expo-background-fetch absent).

minor.issue: Audit log entries for create actions (delivery/customer/driver) store nested MongoDB ObjectId `_id` inside `new_value` because motor's insert_one mutates the dict in-place (adds `_id`) and then `new={k:v for k,v in doc.items() if k!='versions'}` includes it. Caused a one-off 500 (ObjectId not iterable in jsonable_encoder) during the first audit test run; later runs render `_id` as hex string. Fix: strip `_id` (e.g., `doc.pop('_id', None)`) before passing as audit `new_value` OR exclude/serialize ObjectId on response. Locations: server.py create_customer (~L431), create_driver (~L470), create_delivery (~L575).

issues: Console warns: 'shadow*' style props are deprecated, use boxShadow (RN Web). Cosmetic only.
Console warns: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Cosmetic only.

action_items: Strip `_id` (ObjectId) from dicts passed as audit `new_value` in create_customer, create_driver, create_delivery, or pop it after insert. Prevents flaky 500 on /api/audit-logs.
Optional: silence RN Web 'shadow*' and 'pointerEvents' deprecation warnings by migrating to boxShadow / style.pointerEvents in styles.
Optional: backend/server.py is 794 lines — consider splitting into routers/auth.py, routers/deliveries.py, etc. (close to 700-line guideline).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses motor.insert_one which mutates the passed dict to add `_id` (ObjectId). Several places audit the same dict afterwards, leaking ObjectId into audit_logs collection (not strict JSON-safe).
Audit() stores arbitrary nested values; should sanitize ObjectId/datetime to strings to be safe across DB drivers.

deliveries 'duplicate detection' window comment says 'last 5 min' but the query has no time filter — any prior identical row (same date+customer+driver+product+quantity, not deleted) flags duplicate. Either OK (intended) or align comment/behavior.
GET /api/deliveries/trash auto-purges entries older than 30 days on every call (side effect on a read endpoint). Consider moving to a background task.
JWT_SECRET defaults to a hardcoded fallback if env missing — acceptable for dev but should be required in prod (raise if not set).
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed; tests create then delete TEST_-prefixed customers/drivers/deliveries. Pre-existing demo data (ABC Store, Sharma Kirana, XYZ Mart customers; Mahesh, Rajesh drivers; ~6 deliveries) was left untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend at https://delivery-sync-pro.preview.emergentagent.com/api. Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v` for regression. All 25 tests pass. The audit-logs ObjectId issue is latent — only seen once when audit_logs collection contained many create entries with nested ObjectId; fix in main agent recommended. Frontend Expo web verified end-to-end on 390x844 viewport. Demo creds: admin@kushanji.com / Admin@123 and user@kushanji.com / User@123.
rca_of_issue: Root cause of transient 500 on /api/audit-logs: motor's `insert_one(doc)` adds an ObjectId `_id` to the passed dict in-place. Several handlers (create_customer/driver/delivery) call audit() AFTER insert_one with the same dict (or shallow copy of items), so audit_logs documents contain a nested `_id: ObjectId(...)` inside `new_value`. FastAPI's jsonable_encoder may fail with `"'ObjectId' object is not iterable"` on certain dict shapes. Mitigation: `doc_for_audit = {k: v for k, v in doc.items() if k != '_id' and k != 'versions'}` before audit() call, OR `doc.pop('_id', None)` after insert_one. Reproduction: create several deliveries/customers via authenticated POSTs, then GET /api/audit-logs?limit=500 with admin token; observe 500 (sporadic).
